Weekly Diet Plan: Hey Aditya!

Alright, let's get you fueled properly for the next phase. You're juggling 5 gym sessions a week plus 12k steps daily, and you want to drop to 62kg in 12 weeks while keeping your running and lifting strong — that's totally doable, but recovery and nutrition timing are going to be your best friends here. I've built this week around your usual meal times and kept it simple with foods you already enjoy. We're sitting around 2100-2200 kcal daily with solid protein spread across the day to support both your strength work and your tempo runs. Let's dive in.

---

**DAY 1 – Monday**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
Go with 1 plate poha (around 60g raw poha, fluffed up with veggies, curry leaves, peanuts — about 10g peanuts for crunch), or if you want something lighter, 3 idlis with 2 tbsp coconut chutney and a small bowl of sambar (roughly 150ml).
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1 cup cooked rice (about 150g cooked weight), 1 katori dal (approx 150g cooked moong or masoor dal), 1 small katori mixed veg sabzi (beans, carrot, capsicum — around 100g), and a small side of curd (80g).
(P: 18g | C: 68g | F: 6g | ~400 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm — pre-workout)**
Your usual tea with 30g roasted chana, or swap the chana for 1 banana and a small handful of peanuts (15g) if you need quick energy before lifting.
(P: 8g | C: 28g | F: 6g | ~190 kcal)

**Post-Workout (around 7:45 pm, right after gym)**
1 scoop whey protein (25g powder) mixed in 250ml water, plus 1 medium banana.
(P: 26g | C: 28g | F: 2g | ~230 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis (medium, around 30g each), 1 katori paneer bhurji or paneer curry (about 100g paneer), 1 small bowl mixed salad (cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon), and a small katori raita (100g curd-based).
(P: 28g | C: 48g | F: 18g | ~470 kcal)

**Before Bed (optional, if hungry)**
A small glass of warm milk (150ml) with a pinch of haldi — helps with recovery.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**DAY 2 – Tuesday**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
Repeat Monday's breakfast — poha or idlis work great.
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1 cup cooked rice (150g), 1 katori rajma or chole (about 150g cooked), 1 small katori sabzi, and curd (80g). Rajma's a bit denser in protein than regular dal, so it's a nice change mid-week.
(P: 20g | C: 70g | F: 5g | ~410 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Tea with 30g roasted chana, same as Monday.
(P: 8g | C: 28g | F: 6g | ~190 kcal)

**Post-Workout (7:45 pm)**
1 scoop whey + 1 banana.
(P: 26g | C: 28g | F: 2g | ~230 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori soya chunks curry (about 40g dry soya chunks, cooked in tomato-onion gravy — gives you a solid 18g protein from the soya alone), salad, and raita.
(P: 26g | C: 46g | F: 12g | ~410 kcal)

**Before Bed**
Warm milk if needed.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**DAY 3 – Wednesday**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
1 plate poha or 3 idlis — keep it consistent, your body likes routine in the mornings.
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
Let's do curd rice today since you mentioned you love it. 1 cup cooked rice mixed with 150g curd, tempered with mustard seeds, curry leaves, and a pinch of hing. Add 1 small katori dal on the side (100g) and some pickle or papad for flavour.
(P: 16g | C: 65g | F: 7g | ~380 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Tea + 30g roasted chana, or 1 small bowl of sprouts chaat (moong sprouts with onion, tomato, lemon, chaat masala — about 80g sprouts).
(P: 9g | C: 26g | F: 5g | ~180 kcal)

**Post-Workout (7:45 pm)**
1 scoop whey + 1 banana.
(P: 26g | C: 28g | F: 2g | ~230 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori paneer curry (100g paneer), salad, raita. Same structure as Monday — if it works, it works.
(P: 28g | C: 48g | F: 18g | ~470 kcal)

**Before Bed**
Warm milk optional.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**DAY 4 – Thursday**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
Same as previous days — poha or idlis.
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1 cup rice, 1 katori dal (150g), 1 katori sabzi, curd (80g). Thursday's a good day to just keep it simple and let your digestion coast.
(P: 18g | C: 68g | F: 6g | ~400 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Tea + 30g roasted chana.
(P: 8g | C: 28g | F: 6g | ~190 kcal)

**Post-Workout (7:45 pm)**
1 scoop whey + 1 banana.
(P: 26g | C: 28g | F: 2g | ~230 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori mixed dal (moong + masoor, about 150g cooked), 1 small bowl stir-fried bhindi or beans (100g), salad, raita. A little variety in the sabzi keeps it interesting.
(P: 22g | C: 50g | F: 10g | ~390 kcal)

**Before Bed**
Warm milk if you want it.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**DAY 5 – Friday**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
Poha or idlis — you know the drill.
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1 cup rice, 1 katori rajma (150g), 1 katori sabzi, curd. Rajma again because it's dense, filling, and you've got an evening session to fuel.
(P: 20g | C: 70g | F: 5g | ~410 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Tea + 30g roasted chana, or 1 small bowl of boiled chana chaat (black chana with onion, tomato, lemon — about 80g chana).
(P: 9g | C: 27g | F: 5g | ~185 kcal)

**Post-Workout (7:45 pm)**
1 scoop whey + 1 banana.
(P: 26g | C: 28g | F: 2g | ~230 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori paneer bhurji (100g paneer), salad, raita. End the training week strong.
(P: 28g | C: 48g | F: 18g | ~470 kcal)

**Before Bed**
Warm milk optional.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**DAY 6 – Saturday (Rest or Light Activity Day)**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
1 plate poha or 3 idlis with chutney and sambar.
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
Let's do curd rice again — it's Saturday, keep it chill. 1 cup rice + 150g curd, tempered. Add 1 small katori dal (100g) on the side, and maybe some fried papad or pickle.
(P: 16g | C: 65g | F: 7g | ~380 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Tea + 30g roasted chana, or a small handful of peanuts (20g) with 1 small fruit (apple or orange).
(P: 7g | C: 24g | F: 8g | ~190 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori soya chunks curry (40g dry soya), salad, raita. Soya's great on rest days — keeps protein up without feeling too heavy.
(P: 26g | C: 46g | F: 12g | ~410 kcal)

**Before Bed**
Warm milk if you like.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**DAY 7 – Sunday (Rest or Long Walk Day)**

**Breakfast (8:30 am)**
Same as the week — poha or idlis. Consistency is your friend.
(P: 12g | C: 52g | F: 9g | ~340 kcal)

**Lunch (1:30 pm)**
1 cup rice, 1 katori dal (150g), 1 katori sabzi, curd (80g). Sunday lunch, nothing fancy, just solid fuel.
(P: 18g | C: 68g | F: 6g | ~400 kcal)

**Evening Snack (6:30 pm)**
Tea + 30g roasted chana, or sprouts chaat if you prepped some.
(P: 8g | C: 28g | F: 6g | ~190 kcal)

**Dinner (9:00 pm)**
2 rotis, 1 katori paneer curry (100g paneer), salad, raita. Close the week the same way you started — strong and simple.
(P: 28g | C: 48g | F: 18g | ~470 kcal)

**Before Bed**
Warm milk optional.
(P: 5g | C: 7g | F: 6g | ~100 kcal)

---

**A Few Quick Notes:**

- Your post-workout whey + banana is timed right after your evening sessions — that's your recovery window, don't skip it.
- On days you're doing tempo runs or feeling more gassed, you can bump the evening snack to include an extra banana or swap chana for a slightly larger portion (40g). Listen to your body.
- If paneer feels heavy some nights, swap it for soya chunks or extra dal — you'll still hit your protein target.
- Keep your water consistent (2L minimum), and try to sip throughout the day, not all at once before bed.
- I've kept the food budget-friendly and centred around dal, paneer, soya, and chana — all solid, affordable protein sources that fit your 8k/month budget comfortably.

You've got the structure, the fuel, and the training plan in place. Just stay consistent, track how you feel during your runs and lifts, and we'll tweak things in the next check-in if needed. Let's get after it this week, Aditya — you've got this!
